proteins.name: WW domain (Pin1)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Seewald et al., 2007, Nat Struct Mol Biol 14:1120; 278 K, 700 MHz
proteins.notes: Pin1 WW domain extracted from full-length Pin1 X-ray (2F21, 1.50 A, Fip loop-1 mutant). Residues 6-39 extracted. Seewald 2007 S2 is on WT Pin1-WW; Fip mutations are in loop 1 (distant from most backbone amides). Methods-section caveat required. See shared/notes/1.1-pin1ww-extraction.md.

proteins.name: Crambin
proteins.bmrb_reference: No 15N relaxation S2 data published. Stability control protein.
proteins.notes: Stability control only. No S2 comparison planned. 3 SS bonds but not in disulfide test set.

proteins.name: GB3 (Protein G B3 domain)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Hall & Fushman, 2003, JBNMR 27:261; 298 K, 500/600 MHz. Also bmr26845.
proteins.notes: One of the most studied NMR dynamics proteins. T1/T2/NOE in BMRB, S2 derivable.

proteins.name: GB1 (Protein G B1 domain)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Barchi et al., 1994; Hall et al., 2003, JBNMR 27:261; 300 K, 500/600/800 MHz

proteins.name: BPTI
proteins.bmrb_reference: Lienin et al., 1998, JACS 120:9870; Smith et al., 1995; 300 K, 500/600 MHz
proteins.notes: 3 disulfide bonds verified in PDB. Key protein for BioEmu SS integrity test.

proteins.name: CI2 (Chymotrypsin Inhibitor 2)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Shaw et al., 1995, Biochemistry 34:2225-2233 (primary, WT CI2 15N relaxation). BMRB 51234 (Gavrilov & Teilum 2022, Biochemistry) contains I57V mutant T1/T2/NOE relaxation data as secondary cross-check; point-mutant distant from most backbone amides.
proteins.notes: Primary S2 reference is Shaw 1995 (WT CI2) to match PDB 2CI2 (WT). BMRB 51234 is the I57V mutant and serves as secondary cross-check only. Residue 57 is internal; expect small (<0.05) S2 perturbations at most sites. See shared/notes/1.1-audit-remediation.md.

proteins.name: CspA (Cold Shock Protein A)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Feng et al., 1998, Biochemistry 37:10881; 300 K, 600 MHz
proteins.notes: E. coli cold shock protein A. PDB 1MJC is X-ray at 2.00 A (69 modeled residues; N-terminal Met may be trimmed in crystal, manifest residue_count 70 reflects the full-construct sequence used for BMRB S2).

proteins.name: alpha-3D
proteins.bmrb_reference: Walsh et al., 2001, Biochemistry 40:9560; 298 K, 600 MHz
proteins.notes: De novo designed three-helix bundle. 15N, 13C, and 2H relaxation data published.

proteins.name: Calbindin D9k
proteins.bmrb_reference: Akke et al., 1993, Biochemistry 32:9832-9844 (apo-calbindin D9k, primary apo S2 reference). Kordel et al. 1992 Biochemistry 31:4856 is the holo S2 reference and must NOT be used for apo comparison.
proteins.notes: APO state locked. PDB 3ICB is the Ca2+-bound crystal structure; Ca2+ ions and sulfate must be STRIPPED before MD preparation. Use Akke et al. 1993 Biochemistry 32:9832-9844 as the primary apo S2 reference. Kordel et al. 1992 Biochemistry 31:4856 is the holo S2 reference and must NOT be used for apo comparison. See shared/notes/1.1-calbindin-state-lock.md for the Ca-stripping preparation protocol.

proteins.name: Ubiquitin
proteins.bmrb_reference: Tjandra et al., 1995, JACS 117:12562; 300 K, 600 MHz; 126 S2 values in BMRB
proteins.notes: One of the best-characterized NMR dynamics proteins. S2 directly in BMRB.

proteins.name: HPr
proteins.bmrb_reference: None. van Nuland et al. 1995 JMB 246:180 was the prior cited source but is a structure-determination paper (NOE restraints) with no 15N T1/T2/NOE relaxation data and no S2 order parameters. See shared/notes/1.1-hpr-s2-verification.md for full verification.
proteins.notes: Retained for structural and stability comparison only. Excluded from quantitative S2 analysis: van Nuland 1995 JMB 246:180 cited as S2 source but confirmed to contain only NOE structural data; no alternative 15N relaxation S2 source identified in public literature/BMRB as of 2026-04-17. User-approved Option A disposition. Histidine-containing phosphocarrier protein. PDB 1HDN is solution NMR, 30 conformers. Higher-resolution X-ray alternative: 1POH (2.00 A).

proteins.name: Barnase
proteins.bmrb_reference: Sahu et al., 2000; BMRB 26619/26620 with S2 data; 300 K, 600 MHz
proteins.notes: RNase from B. amyloliquefaciens. PDB 1BNR is solution NMR, 20 conformers (not X-ray as previously mislabeled). X-ray alternatives available: 1A2P (1.5 A) or 1BNI (2.1 A). Primary S2: Caro, Valentine, Wand 2017 PNAS (BMRB 26619, 157 order parameters, 308 K, Bruker 500/600/750 MHz). Secondary: Sahu et al. 2000 J Biomol NMR 18:107 (free barnase, 14.1 T, mean S2 = 0.80). Garnet validation-set contamination: barnase-barstar complex (not free barnase) — see shared/notes/1.1-garnet-contamination-plan.md.

proteins.name: HEWL (Hen Egg-White Lysozyme)
proteins.bmrb_reference: BMRB 18304 S2 order parameters; Buck et al., 2007; 308 K, 600 MHz
proteins.notes: 4 disulfide bonds verified in PDB. Key protein for BioEmu SS integrity test.

proteins.name: T4 Lysozyme
proteins.bmrb_reference: Mulder, Hon, Muhandiram, Dahlquist, Kay, 2000, Biochemistry 39(41):12614-12622 (PMID 11027141, DOI 10.1021/bi001351t). Contains side-by-side WT T4L and L99A backbone 15N T1/T2/NOE at 11.7 T and 18.8 T (500, 800 MHz), 298 K. WT subset is the primary S2 reference for PDB 3LZM. Secondary: Meirovitch 2012 J Phys Chem B 116:6118 (SRLS re-analysis, S0^2 = 0.910 +/- 0.046). Methods precedent: Hoffmann et al. 2018 PCCP 20:24577 (MD/NMR comparison).
proteins.notes: Bacteriophage T4 lysozyme WT structure (3LZM). Primary S2 reference locked to Mulder 2000 Biochemistry 39:12614-12622 (WT subset of a paired WT/L99A study, 500 and 800 MHz, 298 K). Earlier manifest citation "Mulder 2001 Biochemistry 40:4458" was a transcription error for the same paper (correct is Biochemistry 39:12614-12622, 2000) and did contain WT data all along; the L99A reference in the same paper is for cavity-mutant comparison only, not primary. Cavity-region residues (~29 of 164) can be stratified in Phase 2 if needed. T4L is Garnet-CLEAN (HEWL is different lysozyme fold family). See shared/notes/1.1-t4l-s2-candidates.md for the full candidate list and reconciliation; supersedes the earlier shared/notes/1.1-t4l-citation-verification.md for the WT-data-presence question.

proteins.name: NTL9 (N-terminal domain of ribosomal protein L9)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Lillemoen & Hoffman, 1998, J Mol Biol 281:539-551 (PubMed 9698568); S2 from 15N T1/T2/NOE Lipari-Szabo model-free. BMRB 17675 contains NTL9 V3A/I4A chemical shifts only (no S2).
proteins.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for T5 margin expansion. Scout report: shared/notes/1.1-t5-margin-candidates.md. 2HBB is 1.90 A X-ray of G. stearothermophilus NTL9; chain A resolved residues 1-51 (functional construct is 1-56 but C-terminal tail residues 52-56 are disordered in the crystal). Zn ions in chains B-E of asymmetric unit; chain A is Zn-free. 3 beta + 2 alpha fold, canonical protein-folding benchmark (Raleigh lab, Shaw lab Anton 2.9 ms trajectory).

proteins.name: ACBP (bovine Acyl-CoA Binding Protein)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Rischel et al., 1994, Biochemistry 33:13997-14002 (PubMed 7947808); 15N T1/T2/NOE for apo- and holo-ACBP. Follow-up: Kragelund et al. 1995 J Mol Biol 250:448-460 and Rogne et al. 2009 BBA 1794:1239-1247 (dispersion and slow dynamics).
proteins.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for T5 margin expansion. Scout report: shared/notes/1.1-t5-margin-candidates.md. 2ABD is the solution NMR apo structure of bovine ACBP (UniProt P07107); model 1 extracted as canonical starting structure, 86 residues, 0 Cys. Four-alpha-helix bundle topology extends fold coverage of benchmark (currently mostly alpha/beta or mostly-beta). Classic Kragelund/Poulsen lab characterization.

proteins.name: FKBP12 (human FK506-binding protein)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Cheng et al., 1993, Biochemistry 32:9000-9010 (PubMed 7690248); 15N T1/T2/NOE for uncomplexed FKBP12, mean S2 ~= 0.88. Follow-up: Sapienza et al., 2011, J Mol Biol 405:378-394 (higher field); Sapienza & Lee, 2018, Biochemistry 57:6482-6495 (side-chain dynamics).
proteins.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for T5 margin expansion. Scout report: shared/notes/1.1-t5-margin-candidates.md. Starting PDB changed from 1FKF (FK506-bound) to 2PPN (0.92 A apo) for consistency with apo-state S2 reference data. 107 aa, 1 Cys at residue 22 (NOT a disulfide bond - single free thiol in core). FKBP fold (alpha/beta with dynamic '80s loop'); tests loop-motion capture by force fields.

proteins.name: Engrailed homeodomain (EnHD)
proteins.bmrb_reference: Religa et al., 2008, J Biomol NMR 40:189-202 (PubMed 18320310); BMRB 15536 contains 58 S2 order parameter entries. 500 MHz (Bruker DRX500), 298 K, pH 5.7.
proteins.notes: Added 2026-04-17 for T5 margin expansion. Scout report: shared/notes/1.1-t5-margin-candidates.md. Only one of the 4 additions with S2 data directly deposited in BMRB (15536). 1ENH is 2.10 A X-ray Drosophila engrailed homeodomain; chain A modeled residues 3-56 (full construct is 1-61 but residues 1-2 and 57-61 are disordered). Three-alpha-helix bundle, DNA-binding protein. Loop 1-2 shows us-ms motion per Religa 2008.

summary.notes: Expanded 2026-04-17: added NTL9, ACBP, FKBP12, EnHD to restore T5 margin after Phase 0 BPTI drop and Subphase 1.1 HEWL drop. Effective benchmark = 16 active proteins (manifest has 18 entries: 14 original minus BPTI (6PTI) and HEWL (1AKI) dropped on AK3 triggers, plus 4 new additions). 14 of 16 active proteins have confirmed BMRB or published 15N relaxation S2 data (Crambin and HPr are retained for structural/stability comparison only: Crambin is the classical stability control; HPr per Option A after van Nuland 1995 JMB 246:180 citation was verified to contain no relaxation data and no alternative WT HPr S2 source was located). T4L S2 reference locked 2026-04-17 to Mulder 2000 Biochemistry 39:12614-12622 (WT + L99A paired data at 500/800 MHz, 298 K). T5 threshold (>=12 of 14 per combined-paper criterion) is met with 2-protein margin: 14 S2-bearing / 16 active. See shared/notes/1.1-protein-count-canonical.md for the authoritative protein-count reconciliation.

dropped_proteins.reason: Phase 0 task-004 BioEmu CB-CB SS integrity 56.1% at 4.5 A (AK3 triggered)
Subphase 1.1 task-005 BioEmu SG-SG integrity 40.2% even with PDBFixer sidechain reconstruction (AK3 triggered)
